candidate_summary: Layered modular memory architecture inspired by Chumash tomol plank canoe: 3-4 stacked modular memory tiers (short-term FIFO, episodic, semantic graph, procedural cache) over a parametric keel base, joined by a learned cross-tier yop-sealant projection plus tok-seam embeddings enabling frameless tier-walking access via cross-tier attention.

results.name: ByteRover Agent-Native Memory LLM-Curated Hierarchical Context (2604.01599)
results.description: LLM-curated hierarchical multi-tier memory with explicit cross-tier projection between tiers.
results.rationale: Hierarchical multi-tier memory with cross-tier projection directly maps to TOMOL's stacked tiers + cross-tier sealant projection. Functional twin on both stacked-tier composition and cross-tier learned projection mechanism.

results.name: A-Mem Agentic Memory LLM Agents (2502.12110)
results.description: Agentic memory architecture combining episodic and semantic stores with cross-tier integration.
results.rationale: Episodic + semantic modular tiers with cross-tier integration cover the TOMOL multi-tier composition + cross-tier access paradigm; matches stacked-tier and cross-tier-attention bridge concepts.

results.name: MIRIX Multi-Agent Memory System LLM Agents (2507.07957)
results.description: Six-component modular memory: Core + Episodic + Semantic + Procedural + Resource + Knowledge Vault.
results.rationale: MIRIX's 6-component modular composition is a strict superset of TOMOL's 3-4 stacked modular tiers (short-term/episodic/semantic/procedural). Exact functional twin on the stacked modular tier composition axis.

results.name: Memory Survey LLM Agentic 2026 (2603.04740)
results.description: Survey of LLM-agent memory architectures including the CoALA episodic/semantic/procedural/working framework.
results.rationale: CoALA's episodic/semantic/procedural/working 4-tier framework is functionally identical to TOMOL's 3-4 stacked modular tier composition. Survey form covers the paradigm.

results.name: MemMachine Ground-Truth-Preserving Personalized AI Agents (2604.04853)
results.description: Personalized agent memory with cross-tier persistence and ground-truth preservation across layers.
results.rationale: Cross-tier persistence preserving ground-truth across layers covers the TOMOL cross-tier sealant paradigm preventing memory leakage; functional saturation on cross-tier persistence mechanism.

results.name: Anatomy of Agentic Memory Taxonomy Evaluation (2602.19320)
results.description: Taxonomy and empirical analysis of agentic memory architectures.
results.rationale: Comprehensive taxonomy of modular tier memory architectures covers the TOMOL composition paradigm; functional saturation on modular memory tier taxonomy.

results.name: M2PA Multi-Memory Planning Agent Open Worlds (ACL 2025)
results.description: Multi-memory planning agent for open worlds with modular memory composition.
results.rationale: Multi-memory modular composition for planning agents covers the TOMOL stacked modular tier paradigm; both semantic and functional scores clear the 0.7 threshold.

results.name: Multi-Layered Memory Architectures LLM Agents (2603.29194)
results.description: Multi-layer memory architectures for LLM agents with experimental evaluation across stacked memory layers.
results.rationale: Explicitly acknowledged by the candidate as the closest prior art. Multi-layered memory for LLM agents is the exact paradigm TOMOL claims (3-4 stacked modular memory tiers). Exact functional twin on the stacked/layered tier composition axis.

decision_rationale: All 8 retrieved papers clear both the semantic (>=0.7) and functional (>=0.7) thresholds, with 3 also clearing keyword overlap (>=2). The prior-art space — ByteRover hierarchical multi-tier with cross-tier projection, MIRIX 6-component modular memory, CoALA 4-tier (episodic/semantic/procedural/working), A-Mem, MemMachine cross-tier persistence, agentic memory taxonomy, M2PA modular memory, and explicitly Multi-Layered Memory 2603.29194 — fully saturates TOMOL-STACK's claimed contribution. The candidate's distinguishing features (cross-tier projection, cross-tier attention, modular tier composition) are already mainstream in the cited prior art, leaving only superficial cultural-analogy relabeling (yop-sealant, tok-seam). Novelty fails; verdict FAIL.
